title: Aviv Regev | Head, Executive Vice President ...
excerpts:
Section Title: Aviv Regev > Head, Executive Vice President, Genentech Research and Early Development
Content:
My group deciphers the molecular circuits that govern cells, tissues and organs in health and their malfunction in disease. Our lab has pioneered foundational experimental and computational methods in single-cell and spatial genomics, working toward greater understanding of the circuits in and function of cells and tissues in health and disease, including autoimmune disease, inflammation, COVID-19 cancer.
Genentech Publications
[A single-nucleus and spatial transcriptomic atlas of the COVID-19 liver reveals topological, functional, and regenerative organ disruption in patients](https://www.scopus.com/inward/record.uri?partnerID=HzOxMe3b&scp=105000178536&origin=inward)
Genome Biology, ISSN: 1474760X 14747596

title: Aviv Regev - Wikipedia
excerpts: **Aviv Regev** ( Hebrew : אביב רגב; born 11 July 1971) <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> is a computational biologist and systems biologist and Executive Vice President and Head of Genentech Research and Early Development in Genentech/Roche. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> She is a core member (on leave) at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard and professor (on leave) at the Department of Biology of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology . <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> Regev is a pioneer of single cell genomics and of computational and systems biology of gene regulatory circuits . She founded and leads the Human Cell Atlas project, <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> together with Sarah Teichmann . Since 2024, she has served as a scientific advisory board member of Arc Institute . <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup>

title: Aviv Regev to Deliver Keynote Address at ISSCR 2026 Annual Meeting in Montréal — International Society for Stem Cell Research
excerpts:
Section Title: Aviv Regev to Deliver Keynote Address at ISSCR 2026 Annual Meeting in Montréal
Content:
Widely recognized as one of the leading life scientists of her generation, Dr. Regev is a pioneering computational and systems biologist whose work has reshaped modern biology. She held senior leadership roles at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard, served as a professor of biology at MIT, and was an Investigator at the Howard Hughes Medical Institute. As a founding co-chair of the [Human Cell Atlas](https://www.humancellatlas.org/) and founding director of the [Klarman Cell Observatory](https://www.broadinstitute.org/klarman-cell-observatory) , Dr. Regev has helped establish transformative single-cell and spatial genomics approaches that are redefining how scientists understand cell, tissue, and organ function in health and disease.

title: Aviv Regev: The Revolution in Digital Biology

Section Title: Ground Truths > Aviv Regev: The Revolution in Digital Biology
Content:
Eric Topol ( [00:05](https://www.rev.com/transcript-editor/shared/KaIeygj1YMumCjZWZUClqBIosvSMykIJ6Bc7BOXstuS7T8N8evM1jGihOyhlI5ydTfZx2PWcOaiNVUx8d3pM3ezKyTg?loadFrom=DocumentDeeplink&ts=5.91) ):
Hello, it's Eric Topol with Ground Truths and with me today I've really got the pleasure of welcoming Aviv Regev, who is the Executive Vice President of Research and Early Development at Genentech, having been 14 years a leader at the Broad Institute and who I view as one of the leading life scientists in the world. So Aviv, thanks so much for joining.
Aviv Regev ( [00:33](https://www.rev.com/transcript-editor/shared/1mz3YMLYCPHGg2Md-LSyn6lSRLyBMqYgTXPdNFkATLhJqZhovKOLByuEL0rS2GyppZMy5svR_WL4WaMByRZncFeTvqo?loadFrom=DocumentDeeplink&ts=33) ):
Thank you for having me and for the very kind introduction.

title: How the Human Cell Atlas is fast-tracking new medicines
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > How the Human Cell Atlas is fast-tracking new medicines
Content:
Developing new medications can take years. But computational biologist Aviv Regev says AI-powered cell mapping is transforming the process—helping to create life-saving drugs in much less time.
**About Aviv Regev**
[Aviv Regev](https://www.gene.com/scientists/our-scientists/aviv-regev) is the Executive Vice President at Genentech Research and Early Development, where she oversees all aspects of drug discovery and development. Before joining Genentech, she held several prominent roles at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard, including Chair of the Faculty and founding director of the Klarman Cell Observatory.
